expectations.text: Navigates to localhost:5173 using available browser tools to inspect the running application
expectations.evidence: Step 2 describes calling mcp__claude_in_chrome__navigate({ url: "http://localhost:5173" }) to load the app. The plan explicitly identifies this as the first action.

expectations.text: Checks the browser console for JavaScript errors or warnings
expectations.evidence: Step 4 describes calling mcp__claude_in_chrome__get_console_logs() to read JS errors and network failures. Categorizes expected error types (TypeError, ReferenceError, 404s, CORS, framework errors).

expectations.text: Identifies specific issues (broken image URLs, JS errors, or similar) from the browser inspection
expectations.evidence: Steps 5-6 describe using mcp__claude_in_chrome__evaluate to inspect img elements (src, complete, naturalWidth) and identify root causes. Four specific scenarios are enumerated: broken URLs, JS runtime errors blocking render, CORS/network errors, and mixed issues.

expectations.text: Makes code changes to fix the identified issues
expectations.evidence: Step 6 describes hypothetical fix scenarios (A through D) and mentions using the Edit tool, but no actual code changes are made. The output is a plan document, not a record of executed actions. No files were edited.

expectations.text: Returns to the browser after fixing to confirm the issues are resolved
expectations.evidence: Step 7 explicitly describes re-verification: taking a fresh screenshot, re-checking console logs, and re-evaluating img elements after fixes. Step 8 adds a broader visual quality check. The iterative loop is clearly stated.
